title: Executive Director of Early Childhood Education
notes: Austin ISD ~73,000 students. Has dedicated Early Childhood Education department (austinisd.org/academics/curriculum/early-childhood). Strong PreK 3/4 program with full-day across most campuses; bilingual/dual-language emphasis. Email format firstname.lastname@austinisd.org standard. Dr. Matias Segura is Supt. HB 3 full-day PreK universal. Active HB 1605 review and TEA-approved IM adoption discussions. Reading Academies fully implemented K-3.

title: Early Childhood Education Program Administrator
notes: Northside ISD (San Antonio) ~100K students - 4th largest in Texas. Domain is nisd.net (district has multiple alias domains). Recently expanded full-day Pre-K to qualifying 3-year-olds, sunset tuition Pre-K post HB2. Barbara Triplett mentioned in district pages as early education program lead. nisdpk_information@nisd.net is official PK contact. Manual enrichment recommended for specific director. Note: input website northsideisd.us appears to be alias - primary is nisd.net.

title: Assistant Superintendent of Academic Services
notes: Plano ISD ~48,000 students. Katrina Hasley is Asst Supt Academic Services overseeing curriculum, ECE. Director of Elementary Curriculum and Director of Early Childhood roles exist beneath. Email format firstname.lastname@pisd.edu standard. Dr. Theresa Williams is Supt. HB 3 full-day PreK across district. Major HB 1605 adoption decisions in progress. Reading Academies K-3 fully implemented.

title: Chief Academic Officer / Asst Supt of Curriculum & Instruction
notes: Garland ISD ~52,000 students (Dallas-area). Has Early Childhood department - PreK at most elementary campuses. Linda Chance oversees C&I. Director of Early Childhood reports up through C&I. Email format firstinitial+lastname@garlandisd.net standard. Dr. Ricardo Lopez is Supt. HB 3 full-day Pre-K universal at qualifying campuses; large bilingual program. Title I supports significant ECE expansion.

title: Associate Superintendent of Academic Services
notes: Pasadena ISD ~52,000 students (Harris County, southeast Houston). Has Early Childhood Education department with multiple ECE/PreK centers. Laura Voigt is Assoc Supt Academic Services. Director of Early Childhood role exists - reports to Academic Services. Email format firstinitial+lastname@pasadenaisd.org. DeeAnn Powell is Supt. HB 3 full-day PreK; large bilingual/dual-language program. Active Reading Academies K-3 cohorts.

title: Executive Director of Early Childhood Education
notes: Spring Branch ISD (west Houston) ~33,000 students. Has dedicated Early Childhood Education department. Bilingual PreK strong; runs Cornerstone Academy and multiple ECE programs. Email format firstname.lastname@springbranchisd.com standard. Dr. Lance Kramer is Supt. HB 3 full-day Pre-K; substantial Pre-K 3 expansion. Active HB 1605 adoption discussions.

title: Chief Academic Officer / Asst Supt of Teaching & Learning
notes: El Paso ISD ~49,000 students. Has Early Childhood department with dedicated PreK coordinator. Tamekia Brown is CAO overseeing curriculum and ECE. Director of Early Childhood reports up through Academic Services. Email format firstinitiallastname@episd.org. Dr. Diana Sayavedra is Supt. HB 3 full-day Pre-K universal; large dual-language program. Border district with high ED enrollment. Active HB 1605 review.

title: Deputy Superintendent of Academics
notes: Corpus Christi ISD ~33,000 students. Has Early Childhood Education department - Roy Miller HS ECE programs and dedicated PreK campuses. Cissy Reynolds-Perez is Deputy Supt Academics. Director of Early Childhood reports through Academics. Email format firstname.lastname@ccisd.us. Dr. Roland Hernandez is Supt. HB 3 full-day Pre-K; large bilingual program. Coastal Bend - significant ED population.

title: Executive Director of Curriculum & Instruction
notes: Laredo ISD ~22,000 students (border district). Has Early Childhood/PreK programs at most elementary campuses; heavy bilingual emphasis. Director of C&I oversees ECE. Email format firstinitial+lastname@laredoisd.org. Dr. Sylvia Rios is Supt. HB 3 full-day Pre-K with significant federal Title I and bilingual funding. Reading Academies cohort completion. Active TEA-approved IM adoption.

title: Administrator for Early Childhood Education
notes: Brownsville ISD ~38,000 students (Rio Grande Valley/border). Has dedicated Early Childhood Education department with multiple PreK centers. Strong bilingual emphasis. Reading Academies fully implemented K-3.

Fast-growing district with active facilities planning.
